Atty. WILLIAM LIM, Esq. of Wong Fleming, P.C. (Tel: 609-951-9520) for consolidated plaintiff/cross-defendant Key Equipment Finance;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF KEY EQUIPMENT FINANCE'S Motion To Seal Documents In Connection With Amended Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Key Equipment Finance's unopposed motion to seal is granted. The court finds that (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the records, (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the records, (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the records are not sealed, (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Order signed.

Notice Of Motion, Motion To Dismiss Basbd On Forum Non Conveniens, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And Declaration Of Andrew C. Shen
Off Calendar Jun-06-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Filomena Meyer, Esq. 310-909-8000 of Hinshaw & Culbertson LLP for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Andrew Shen, Esq. 202-326-7900 of Kellogg Hansen Todd Figel & Frederick for Defendant COGNIZANT WORLDWIDE LIMITED, COGNIZANT TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS CORPORATION.

RULING - A R G U E D.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT COGNIZANT WORLDWIDE LIMITED, COGNIZANT TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS CORPORATION's Motion To Dismiss Based On Forum Non Conveniens.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants Cognizant Worldwide Ltd. and Cognizant Technology Solutions Corp.'s motion to dismiss based on forum non conveniens is GRANTED. A mandatory forum-selection clause "will ordinarily be given effect without any analysis of convenience." (Intershop Communications v. Sup. Ct. (2002) 104 Cal.App.4th 191, 196.) It need not be determined whether the forum-selection clause in the master services agreement (New York) or the statement of work (Washington) applies; both are mandatory clauses that require dismissal of plaintiff Barrett Business Services, Inc.'s claims against the Cognizant entities in California. Intershop provides that a mandatory forum-selection clause need not say the forum is "exclusive;" rather, use of "the" connotes "a single place" agreed to be the dispute-resolution forum. (Id.) Whether the exclusive forum is New York or Washington, it is not California.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
